steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The previous sentence states that modern via ferratas are ‘rarely designed to simply reach a summit.’ The sentence after the blank says new routes favor recreation over utility. The transition must connect those two ideas — showing what modern routes do instead, not contradicting or simply adding to the prior claim.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Nonetheless,': signals a contrast or concession, but no contradiction exists between the two sentences. ✗
  • Choice B — 'On the other hand,': also signals contrast between two opposing positions, which is not the relationship here. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Additionally,': adds a new, parallel point, but the second sentence is not a separate fact — it explains what ‘rarely summit-focused’ means in practice. ✗
  • Choice D — 'More often,': shows what is now typical, completing the shift from the rare (summit-focused) to the common (recreation-focused). ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
